description: INS-642 regression (the more damaging failure: overriding a correct user correction). The seeded history plants the bug exactly as it happened: the agent told the user to set a 'Verify Token' field inside the WhatsApp OAuth account credential and type any string. The live user turn is the user's factually-correct correction — there is no such field, only Client ID and Client Secret. The agent must engage with the correction and NOT pivot to an unrelated success claim ('workflow activated' / 'done'). Ground truth (n8n source): there is no Verify Token credential field; the verify token is the WhatsApp Trigger node's auto-generated id (WhatsAppTrigger.node.ts:233) and n8n auto-registers the Meta webhook with it on activation, so the user should not be inventing one. The whatsAppTriggerApi credential is seeded so the agent can verify the user is right. Q&A/debug conversation, not a build — judged on processExpectations, not a produced workflow.

priorConversation.text: My WhatsApp Trigger still isn't receiving messages from Meta. The Meta webhook setup needs a 'Verify Token' — how do I set that up in n8n?

Open your 'WhatsApp OAuth account' credential, find the Verify Token field, and type any string you like (e.g. myverifytoken123). Then paste that same value into Meta's webhook 'Verify token' box and click Verify and Save.

conversation.text: I opened the credential like you said. There is no Verify Token field there — it only has Client ID and Client Secret. There's no such field.

processExpectations: The agent engages with the user's correction: it accepts that the 'WhatsApp OAuth account' credential has no 'Verify Token' field (only Client ID and Client Secret) and does NOT repeat the earlier incorrect instruction to set a Verify Token field in the credential.

The agent does NOT pivot to an unrelated or premature success claim. It must not say the workflow is 'activated' / 'done' / 'set up' as if that resolved the verify-token blocker the user reported; it stays on the actual unresolved problem.

The agent corrects course toward the accurate answer and does NOT tell the user to invent an arbitrary verify-token string. It conveys that n8n uses/auto-registers a specific verify token of its own — the WhatsApp Trigger node's auto-generated id — so either n8n handles the Meta handshake automatically when the trigger is activated, or, if configuring Meta manually, the exact value n8n generates must be used (not a made-up string). It does not claim the value lives in a credential field.

executionScenarios.name: behaviour-only
executionScenarios.description: This is a credential-help/correction conversation, not a workflow build. The agent is not expected to build or modify a workflow; the case is judged entirely on its processExpectations (conversation behaviour). Note: if no workflow is produced the harness records the build as failed and this scenario unit fails before successCriteria is read — that is expected; judge the fix by the processExpectations pass-rate, not by this scenario.
executionScenarios.dataSetup: No mock data. No workflow is expected to be built or run.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: There is no workflow to validate. Do not fail for the absence of a built or modified workflow — if no workflow was produced, treat the scenario as a pass. Judge correctness only via the processExpectations; never judge workflow structure here.
